task_completion.description: The photograph is opened in an image editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image editing application (e.g., GIMP) is open and displaying a photograph (either a real photo or a synthetic image with shapes) on the canvas.

task_completion.description: The artistic oil painting filter tool is accessed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box for 'Oilify', 'Oil painting', or a similar artistic filter is open in the image editor.

task_completion.description: The photograph is transformed into an artistic oil painting.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image on the canvas visibly exhibits an oil painting effect, characterized by brush stroke textures, smoothed edges, and reduced local detail compared to the original photograph.

task_completion.description: The export or save dialog is initiated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export Image', 'Save As', or similar dialog box is open in the image editor.

task_completion.description: The output is exported to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The export dialog shows the file name set to 'output.png' and the destination folder set to '/home/ga/', OR a file manager/terminal verifies the existence of 'output.png' in the '/home/ga/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical image editor rather than terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with a GUI application (like GIMP) to apply the filter, rather than terminal windows running ImageMagick or other CLI image processing commands.

integrity.description: The agent used an actual oil painting filter rather than manual approximations.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent opens and configures an artistic filter dialog (like GIMP's Oilify) rather than manually painting over the image or just applying a generic blur.

integrity.description: The agent exported the file through the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent uses the application's export/save functionality rather than just renaming an existing file or moving a pre-computed image in the terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original image is typically an Unsplash photograph (or a synthetic 800x600 image with colored circles and a rectangle if the download failed). The target effect is an 'Oilify' filter (in GIMP, found under Filters -> Artistic -> Oilify) which creates a brush-stroke appearance by smoothing edges and reducing local color variance. A mask radius of 7-10 pixels is recommended for a moderate effect. The final output must be saved exactly to the path /home/ga/output.png.
